title: senna leiophylla
intro: Senna leiophylla, also known as the hairy senna, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to South Africa. It is a shrub with bright yellow flowers and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its laxative properties. The medicinal properties of Senna leiophylla include anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation. Herbal preparations of Senna leiophylla can be found in tea, capsule, and powder forms, often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
